git_commits: feat(bench): M2.2 bench harness — criterion benches, regression gate, baselines schema
feat(bench): baselines.json, CI jobs, BENCHMARKS.md, DESIGN.md update
public_apis_implemented: pub fn make_saxpy_inputs(n: usize, seed: u64) -> (Vec<f32>, Vec<f32>, f32)
pub fn make_vector_add_inputs(n: usize, seed: u64) -> (Vec<f32>, Vec<f32>)
pub fn saxpy_cpu_reference(x: &[f32], y: &[f32], alpha: f32) -> Vec<f32>
pub fn assemble_saxpy_push_constants(plan: &ParamBindingPlan, n: u32, alpha: f32) -> Vec<u8>
pub fn assemble_vector_add_push_constants(plan: &ParamBindingPlan, n: u32) -> Vec<u8>
cargo_bench_status: compile + cpu_reference complete; dispatch skipped (no AXC_ENABLE_GPU_BENCHES=1 on this runner)
spirv_val_status: n/a (no new SPIR-V codegen in M2.2)
fault_injection_status: AT-714 PASS — fault-injection (bench_regression_fixture_slowdown) correctly triggers exit=1 with 2 regression messages containing cpu_saxpy_
regression_gate_status: PASS — AXC_ENABLE_BENCH_REGRESSION=1 cargo test --release passes without fault-injection
baselines_machine: Intel i9-14900KF + NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell (Linux, rustc 1.94.1)

self_assessment.spec_coverage: All 29 ATs implemented. All [[bench]] entries, all helper functions, regression gate, fault-injection, baselines schema v1 with BTreeMap ordering, CI jobs (AT-714b two-job discipline).
known_limitations: Regression gate baselines are from cargo bench (release-optimized profile); CI regression gate runs cargo test --release to match. If CI uses cargo test (debug), timings will differ significantly from baselines — this is why the CI job explicitly uses --release.
dispatch_gpu benches produce empty timing entries in baselines.json when no AXC_ENABLE_GPU_BENCHES=1 during bench run. Lavapipe numbers require running benches with GPU env vars set.
cpu_saxpy_1024 baseline (288ns from Criterion) may appear as 'possible speedup' in the regression gate on the same machine due to Criterion's finer sampling methodology vs 11-sample median.
deviations: Criterion writes bench output to target/criterion/<bench_name>/ (flat), not target/criterion/<binary>/<bench_name>/. postprocess.rs KNOWN_BENCHES table corrected from the spec's assumed hierarchical layout.

AT-714b fault-injection: vector_add benches may show 'possible speedup' (not regression) because the slowdown feature only affects saxpy_cpu_reference. This is correct per spec — only saxpy benches are slowed by the accumulator loop.
